pages.raw_text:
The expos ires Of the employment
agencies made by the Press stirred
the councilmen to action last night.
j. f. Foster introduced an ordinance
which is strict one and repeats the
former ordinance relating to employ
ment bureaus.


The ordinance introduced by Mr..
Foster provides for licensing and reg
ulating employment offices and pro-
vides for penalty for the violation
thereof. No person shall conduct an
employment Office without a license
from the city comptroller and $40
600 bond. Any person hereafter de-
string to open an employment Office
shall apply in writing to the city coun-
ell and shall state definitely the build
ing OF place where said Office is to be
opened. The petition shall state the
name of the owner OF owners, the class
of business to be conducted, and that
the said owners have never been cin-
victed of any violation of the ordi-
nance relating to employment offices.
and that their license has never been
revoked or forfeited.


The license shall not be granted un-
til the petitioners shall pay the city
treasurer the sum of $3.50 per year.


The petition shall be accompanied
by bond in penal sum of $40 1000. The
bond must be approved by the mayor
and the corporation counsel. The bond
shall be forfeited if money is received
for obtaining position where no po
sitien is procured.


The applicant for license will fur
Their pay such judgments as may be re-
covered against him by reason of wilful
Misrepresentation or deceiving any per-
son transacting any business with such
applicant.


Any person or persons who shall di-
rectly or indirectly make use of i'm
proper devices, deceit or false pre-
lenses for the purpose of obtaining a
fee from any customer, or obtain or at
tempt to obtain employment for per-
son for any immoral or illegal employ
ment shall upon conviction he liable to
fine of not less than SIS or more
than 800.
